title: Where should you start?
intro: A short Croatian check that gets harder as it goes, so we can start you on the right day of the course. It takes about two minutes and you can retake it any time.

questions.prompt: 'Ja ___ Ana.' (I am Ana)
questions.explanation: The verb biti in the first person singular is sam: ja sam, ti si, on je.

questions.prompt: How do you greet someone in the morning?
questions.explanation: Dobro jutro is the morning greeting; the others mean good evening, good night and goodbye.

questions.prompt: Which Croatian letter sounds like the 'ch' in 'church'?
questions.explanation: č is the ch sound, while c is pronounced ts, š is sh and ž is like the s in 'measure'.

questions.prompt: 'Mi ___ iz Hrvatske.' (We are from Croatia)
questions.explanation: The first person plural of biti is smo.

questions.prompt: 'On ___ hrvatski.' (He speaks Croatian)
questions.explanation: Third person singular of an i-verb ends in -i: on govori.

questions.prompt: Which word means 1000 in standard Croatian?
questions.explanation: Croatian uses tisuća for 1000; stotina is a hundred and milijun is a million.

questions.prompt: 'Čitam ___.' (I am reading a book: knjiga)
questions.explanation: A feminine noun in -a takes -u as a direct object in the accusative.

questions.prompt: '___ stanuješ?' (Where do you live?)
questions.explanation: Gdje asks about location, while kamo asks about direction of movement.

questions.prompt: Which day comes right after utorak?
questions.explanation: Wednesday is srijeda in Croatian; the form sreda belongs to the ekavian variant.

questions.prompt: A woman says 'Yesterday I was at the cinema.' 'Jučer sam ___ u kinu.'
questions.explanation: The past participle agrees with the speaker, so a female speaker says bila.

questions.prompt: 'U torbi imam pet ___.' (five books)
questions.explanation: Numbers from five upwards take the genitive plural, which here has a zero ending.

questions.prompt: 'Sinoć sam bio ___ koncertu.' (at a concert)
questions.explanation: Events such as a concert take na with the locative, not u.

questions.prompt: 'Sutra ___ raditi.' (Tomorrow I will work)
questions.explanation: The future is formed with ću, ćeš, će plus the infinitive.

questions.prompt: 'Dajem ___ knjigu.' (I am giving my brother a book: brat)
questions.explanation: The indirect object stands in the dative, which for masculine nouns ends in -u.

questions.prompt: What is the comparative of 'dobar' (good)?
questions.explanation: Dobar has the irregular comparative bolji; najbolji is the superlative.

questions.prompt: '___ se sviđa ovaj film.' (I like this film)
questions.explanation: Sviđati se takes the dative of the person who likes something.

questions.prompt: 'Marko voli ___ posao.' (Marko loves his own job)
questions.explanation: When the possessor is the subject of the sentence, Croatian uses svoj.

questions.prompt: 'Idem u kazalište ___ prijateljem.' (with a friend)
questions.explanation: Accompaniment is expressed with s plus the instrumental.

questions.prompt: 'Svako jutro ___ novine.' (Every morning I read the paper)
questions.explanation: A repeated habitual action takes the imperfective verb čitati.

questions.prompt: 'To je čovjek ___ sam vidio jučer.' (the man whom I saw yesterday)
questions.explanation: The relative pronoun is the object of vidjeti, so an animate masculine takes the accusative kojeg.

questions.prompt: 'Bez ___ ne mogu raditi.' (without a computer: računalo)
questions.explanation: The preposition bez always governs the genitive.

questions.prompt: 'Trgovina je ___ nedjeljom.' (The shop is closed on Sundays)
questions.explanation: The passive participle agrees with the feminine noun trgovina.

questions.prompt: '___ jezika traži vrijeme.' (Learning a language takes time)
questions.explanation: The verbal noun učenje works as the subject, whereas učenik means pupil.

questions.prompt: 'Da imam vremena, pomogao ___ ti.' (If I had time, I would help you)
questions.explanation: The conditional in the first person singular is bih, placed right after the participle.

questions.prompt: Which sentence has the correct word order for 'She gave it to me'?
questions.explanation: Clitics cluster in second position in the order dative, accusative, then the auxiliary je last.

questions.prompt: 'Rekao je da ___ sutra.' (He said he would come tomorrow)
questions.explanation: Croatian keeps the tense of the original words in reported speech, so the future stays future.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I don't know whether he is coming'?
questions.explanation: An indirect yes or no question puts the verb first with the particle li after it.
